reasoning: Historically, bringing down an entire federal domain is rare. While the Trump administration has removed specific pieces of climate information (like the 2024 Climate Literacy Guide, which was taken down in Feb 2025), they have not shown a pattern of deleting major domains outright. Climate.gov survived the first Trump term. Moreover, recent evidence shows the site is actively being maintained and updated by NOAA; it published new content such as an article on mountain glaciers as recently as May 9, 2025, and provided a climate outlook in March. Given that the resolution date is only about six weeks past the forecast cutoff date (May 21, 2025), the chance of a complete domain shutdown in that short window is very low. There remains a small risk of sudden executive action or random technical downtime on the day of checking, so I am capping the probability at 92%.
